investmentThesis.bullCase: Iron ore prices remain elevated above $100/t driven by Chinese stimulus and supply constraints from major miners. Fenix achieves record production of 4.5Mt at low costs, generating substantial free cash flow. The company continues its progressive dividend policy, potentially paying 4-5 cps annually. Net cash position grows to $100M+, enabling strategic M&A or special dividends. Re-rating occurs as the market recognises the earnings quality and cash generation capability.
investmentThesis.baseCase: Iron ore prices moderate to $95-105/t range as supply normalises. Fenix consistently delivers 4.0-4.2Mt production at AISC of ~$82/wmt. Steady cash generation supports 4 cps annual dividend (8%+ yield). Balance sheet remains debt-free (net cash). The stock trades at 4-5x EV/EBITDA, a discount to larger peers, reflecting its smaller size and single-asset risk.
investmentThesis.bearCase: Iron ore prices fall below $80/t due to a sharp slowdown in Chinese property and infrastructure spending. Fenix' high-cost position relative to majors ($68/t cash cost vs BHP ~$40/t) compresses margins severely. Production guidance is cut. Dividend is reduced or suspended. The stock re-rates down to 3x EBITDA or below. At $70/t iron ore, FEX could trade at $0.30-0.35.

catalysts.title: Q3 2025 Production Report
catalysts.description: Quarterly production and cash cost update. Market expects 1.05-1.1Mt.

catalysts.title: Dividend Ex-Date
catalysts.description: Expected 2.0 cps fully franked dividend for H1 FY25.

catalysts.title: FY2025 Full-Year Results
catalysts.description: Annual results with production, costs, earnings and dividend declaration.

catalysts.title: Iron Ridge Extension Study
catalysts.description: Potential resource extension and mine life update at Iron Ridge.

risks.title: Iron Ore Price Decline
risks.description: A sustained fall in iron ore prices below cash cost would render operations unprofitable.
risks.mitigation: Low cost base ($68/t FOB) provides buffer; industry-low capex requirements

risks.title: Single Asset Concentration
risks.description: Iron Ridge is the sole producing asset; any operational disruption impacts 100% of revenue.
risks.mitigation: Management experienced in WA iron ore; orebody is simple, low strip ratio

risks.title: AUD/USD Appreciation
risks.description: A strengthening AUD reduces AUD-denominated revenues from USD-priced iron ore.
risks.mitigation: Costs also primarily AUD-denominated, providing natural hedge

risks.title: Mining Approvals
risks.description: Changes to mining regulations, royalties, or environmental approvals in WA.
risks.mitigation: All current approvals in place; strong relationships with state government

researchNotes.title: FEX: High-Grade Iron Ore at a Discount
researchNotes.author: Macquarie Research
researchNotes.summary: Fenix remains one of the cheapest iron ore exposure in Australia at 3.8x EV/EBITDA. Strong free cash flow generation supports 8%+ dividend yield. Upgrade to Outperform with $0.75 price target.

researchNotes.title: Iron Ridge Delivering Consistent Results
researchNotes.author: Ord Minnett
researchNotes.summary: Fenix continues to deliver consistent production from Iron Ridge. Costs remain well controlled. Balance sheet is in outstanding shape with net cash position. Maintain Buy.

researchNotes.title: FEX: Valuation Update Post HY Results
researchNotes.author: Canaccord Genuity
researchNotes.summary: HY25 results were in line with expectations. Dividend of 2.0 cps was a positive surprise. Near-term catalysts limited but valuation remains undemanding. Hold rating maintained.
